q.en: Who has taken over Toad Hall?

options.en: The Wild Wooders — weasels, ferrets, and stoats
options.zh: 野林居民——黄鼠狼、雪貂和鼬

options.en: The River Bankers according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 河岸居民（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Rat asks, 'that you've heard nothing about the Stoats and Weasels?' and explains the Wild Wooders 'have been and taken Toad Hall.'
explain.zh: 河鼠问“你没听说鼬和黄鼠狼的事？”并说明野林居民“已经占了蟾蜍府”。

q.en: How did the Wild Wooders capture Toad Hall?
q.zh: 野林居民是如何攻占蟾蜍府的？

options.en: Weasels took the front, ferrets the backyard, stoats the conservatory, by surprise at night
options.zh: 黄鼠狼攻正面、雪貂抄后院、鼬占暖房，趁夜突袭

options.en: A fire forced the animals out according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 一场大火把动物逼走（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: On a dark, stormy night, 'a band of weasels... crept up the carriage drive,' ferrets took the backyard, and stoats occupied the conservatory and billiard room.
explain.zh: 在一个漆黑暴风雨夜，“一队黄鼠狼摸上车道”，雪貂占了后院，鼬控制了暖房和台球室。

q.en: What happens when Toad tries to enter by boat through the creek?
q.zh: 蟾蜍想从小河汊划船进入时发生了什么？

options.en: Stoats drop a stone from the bridge and sink his boat
options.zh: 鼬从桥上扔石头，砸沉了他的船

options.en: Rat saves him in time according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 河鼠及时救了他（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Passing under the bridge, 'Crash! A great stone, dropped from above, smashed through the bottom of the boat. It filled and sank.'
explain.zh: 船行到桥下，“哗啦！一块大石从上方落下，砸穿船底。船进水沉了。”

q.en: How does Toad make up with Rat after losing his boat?
q.zh: 弄丢河鼠的船后，蟾蜍如何与之和解？

options.en: He apologizes fully and promises no action without Rat's advice
options.zh: 他诚恳道歉，并承诺未经河鼠同意不再擅动

options.en: He pays Rat money according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他赔了河鼠钱（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: Toad 'admitted his errors... and made a full apology,' ending with, 'I will be humble and submissive, and will take no action without your kind advice.'
explain.zh: 蟾蜍“承认错误……充分道歉”，最后说：“我会谦卑顺从，没有你的好主意绝不擅动。”

q.en: In the glossary, the word 'ignominiously' (as in 'ignominiously flung into the water') means —
q.zh: 词汇表中，'ignominiously'（如“可耻地丢进水里”）意思是——

options.en: bravely and boldly according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 勇敢而大胆地（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: in a shameful or humiliating manner

explain.en: The glossary defines ignominiously as 'in a shameful, humiliating, or disgraceful manner,' as Rat notes Toad was flung in by a woman.
explain.zh: 词汇表把 ignominiously 解释为“以可耻、丢脸或蒙羞的方式”，河鼠正说蟾蜍被女人丢进了水里。

q.en: The title 'Like Summer Tempests Came His Tears' is a metaphor comparing Toad's tears to —
q.zh: 标题“如夏日骤雨，他的泪来袭”把蟾蜍的泪比作——

options.zh: 突然而暴风骤雨般的情感爆发

options.en: only a gentle rain according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 仅仅一场细雨（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Like summer storms that arrive suddenly, Toad's weeping breaks out in bursts ('a large tear welled up... plop! plop!'), swift and overwhelming.
explain.zh: 如同夏日骤雨突至，蟾蜍的泪也一阵阵迸发（“大泪珠涌出……扑通！扑通！”），急促而汹涌。

q.en: Rat's scolding of Toad suggests the theme that —
q.zh: 河鼠训斥蟾蜍，暗示了主题——

options.en: friends should only flatter according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 朋友只该奉承（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: true friends tell hard truths and Toad needs their guidance
options.zh: 真正的朋友会说逆耳真话，蟾蜍需要他们引导

explain.en: Rat lists all Toad's humiliations 'all because you must needs go and steal a motorcar,' showing friendship means honest correction.
explain.zh: 河鼠数落蟾蜍种种丢脸，“全因为你偏要去偷汽车”，说明友谊意味着诚恳的规劝。

q.en: Mole and Badger's loyalty (watching the house, planning) shows —
q.zh: 鼹鼠和獾的忠诚（守宅、谋划）说明——

options.en: they wanted the house for themselves according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他们想霸占房子（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: they are devoted friends who kept faith in Toad's return
options.zh: 他们是忠贞的朋友，始终相信蟾蜍会回来

explain.en: Rat says those two 'stuck out, through thick and thin, that you would come back again,' camping out to guard the property.
explain.zh: 河鼠说那两位“无论怎样都坚持你会回来”，还露营守着宅子。

q.en: The author gives Toad both vanity and a good heart to —
q.zh: 作者让蟾蜍既有虚荣又有好心肠，是为了——

options.en: teach a lesson about rats according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 上一堂关于老鼠的课（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: make him a lovable, flawed character readers can forgive
options.zh: 塑造一个可爱、有缺点、读者能原谅的角色

explain.en: Toad is 'a thoroughly good-hearted animal and never minded being jawed by those who were his real friends,' balancing fault and warmth.
explain.zh: 蟾蜍“是个彻底好心的动物，从不在真正的朋友教训他时介意”，使缺点与温情并存。

q.en: The detail of Toad sniggering when Mole and Badger were beaten shows —
q.zh: 蟾蜍在鼹鼠和獾被打时窃笑这一细节，说明——

options.en: his selfishness and inability to take others' suffering seriously
options.zh: 他的自私，以及无法认真对待他人的苦难

options.en: his kindness according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他的善良（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: Even hearing his friends were 'beaten severely with sticks,' Toad 'broke into a snigger,' exposing his self-centeredness.
explain.zh: 即便听说朋友“被棍棒痛打”，蟾蜍却“噗嗤窃笑”，显出其以自我为中心。

prompt.en: Toad's friends stood by him even when he was foolish. Tell about a friend or family member who stayed loyal to you through a mistake you made.
prompt.zh: 蟾蜍的朋友即便在他犯傻时也守着他。说说曾有哪位朋友或家人在你犯错时仍对你不离不弃。

en: Describe the mistake and who stayed with you.
Explain why their loyalty mattered to you.
zh: 描述那次错误，以及谁陪在你身边。
说明他们的忠诚对你为何重要。
